meaning.en: Liver
meaning.ar: الكبد

terms.etymology: Greek: hêpar (ἧπαρ) — liver. Related to 'hepatic'.

meaning.en: Pertaining to the liver
meaning.ar: متعلق بالكبد

terms.etymology: Greek: hepatikos — of the liver.

meaning.en: 8-segment surgical classification of the liver
meaning.ar: التصنيف الجراحي الثماني للكبد

terms.etymology: Named after Claude Couinaud (1922–2008), French surgeon and anatomist.

meaning.en: Bladder / sac / pouch
meaning.ar: مثانة / كيس / جراب

terms.etymology: Latin: vesica — bladder. Used in vesica biliaris (gallbladder) and vesica urinaria (urinary bladder).

meaning.en: Relating to bile
meaning.ar: متعلق بالصفراء

terms.etymology: Latin: bilis — bile. From Greek: kholê. Bilirubin derives from the same root.

meaning.en: Gallbladder (prefix in medical terms)
meaning.ar: المرارة (بادئة في المصطلحات الطبية)

terms.etymology: Greek: kholê (bile) + kystis (bladder/sac). Cholecystitis = gallbladder inflammation.

meaning.en: Containing bile — refers to common bile duct
meaning.ar: حاوٍ للصفراء — يشير إلى القناة الصفراوية المشتركة

terms.etymology: Greek: kholê (bile) + dekhomai (to receive/contain). Choledocholithiasis = CBD stones.

meaning.en: All flesh / entirely of flesh
meaning.ar: كل اللحم / لحمي بالكامل

terms.etymology: Greek: pan (all) + kreas (flesh). Named for its fleshy appearance without a prominent capsule.

meaning.en: Main pancreatic duct
meaning.ar: القناة البنكرياسية الرئيسية

terms.etymology: Named after Johann Georg Wirsung (1589–1643), German anatomist who first described the main pancreatic duct.

meaning.en: Endocrine clusters in pancreas producing insulin and glucagon
meaning.ar: مجموعات الخلايا الصمّاء في البنكرياس التي تنتج الأنسولين والجلوكاجون

terms.etymology: Named after Paul Langerhans (1847–1888), German pathologist who described them in 1869.

meaning.en: Spleen
meaning.ar: الطحال

terms.etymology: Latin: lien — spleen. Also Greek: splên (σπλήν). Both roots used: lienal artery / splenic artery.

meaning.en: Enlargement of the spleen (>12 cm on US)
meaning.ar: ضخامة الطحال (> ١٢ سم بالموجات فوق الصوتية)

terms.etymology: Greek: splên (spleen) + megas (large). Clinically defined as long-axis >12 cm on ultrasound.

meaning.en: Notch or depression where vessels/ducts enter an organ
meaning.ar: الشقّ أو المنخفض حيث تدخل الأوعية والقنوات للعضو

terms.etymology: Latin: hilum — a small thing, trifle. Anatomically: the entry/exit point for vessels, nerves, lymphatics.

meaning.en: Kidney (singular)
meaning.ar: الكلية (مفردة)

terms.etymology: Latin: ren (pl. renes). Greek: nephros (νεφρός). Renal = from Latin; nephrotic/nephritis = from Greek.

meaning.en: CT phase (90–120s) when kidney parenchyma enhances homogeneously
meaning.ar: مرحلة CT (٩٠–١٢٠ ثانية) حين يتباين حمة الكلية بشكل منتظم

terms.etymology: Greek: nephros (kidney) + graphein (to write/record). Key phase for detecting renal masses.

meaning.en: Above the kidney — another name for adrenal
meaning.ar: فوق الكلية — اسم آخر للكظرية

terms.etymology: Latin: supra (above) + renalis (renal/kidney). Synonymous with adrenal (Latin: ad = near + ren = kidney).

meaning.en: Mineralocorticoid hormone from zona glomerulosa; regulates Na+/K+
meaning.ar: هرمون قشراني معدني من المنطقة الكبيبية؛ ينظّم الصوديوم والبوتاسيوم

terms.etymology: From aldehyde + sterol + ketone — describing its chemical structure. Discovered 1952 by Sylvia Simpson.

meaning.en: Glucocorticoid from zona fasciculata; stress hormone
meaning.ar: قشراني سكري من المنطقة المحزوزة؛ هرمون الإجهاد

terms.etymology: From cortex + sterol + -ol (alcohol group). Primary stress hormone with immunosuppressive effects.

meaning.en: The great artery from the heart
meaning.ar: الشريان العظيم الخارج من القلب

terms.etymology: Greek: aortê (αορτή) — from aeirein, to lift or carry. Aristotle used this term.

meaning.en: Pertaining to the abdomen / belly
meaning.ar: متعلق بالبطن

terms.etymology: Greek: koilia (κοιλία) — belly, hollow. Celiac axis = first unpaired branch of abdominal aorta at T12.

meaning.en: Pertaining to the mesentery (SMA or IMA)
meaning.ar: متعلق بالمساريقا (الشريان المساريقي العلوي أو السفلي)

terms.etymology: Greek: mesos (middle) + enteron (intestine). Mesentery = the fan-shaped fold suspending the bowel.

meaning.en: Vein
meaning.ar: وريد

terms.etymology: Latin: vena — vein, blood vessel. Root of 'venous', 'venipuncture'. Greek equivalent: phleps (φλέψ).

meaning.en: Hollow / cavity
meaning.ar: مجوّف / تجويف

terms.etymology: Latin: cavus — hollow. Vena cava = hollow vein. Refers to the large lumen of these great veins.

meaning.en: Gate / door / entrance
meaning.ar: بوابة / مدخل

terms.etymology: Latin: porta — gate. Porta hepatis = 'gate of the liver' where portal vein, hepatic artery, and bile duct enter.

meaning.en: Flow towards the liver (normal portal flow direction)
meaning.ar: تدفق نحو الكبد (الاتجاه الطبيعي للوريد البابي)

terms.etymology: Greek: hêpar (liver) + Latin: petere (to seek/move toward). Opposite: hepatofugal (away from liver = abnormal).

meaning.en: Flow away from the liver (abnormal — portal hypertension sign)
meaning.ar: تدفق بعيداً عن الكبد (غير طبيعي — علامة ارتفاع ضغط باب الكبد)

terms.etymology: Greek: hêpar (liver) + Latin: fugere (to flee). Reversal of portal flow = serious sign.

meaning.en: Stomach / belly
meaning.ar: المعدة / البطن

terms.etymology: Greek: gastêr (γαστήρ) — stomach. Root of gastric, gastritis, gastroscopy, gastroenterology.

meaning.en: Gatekeeper — the muscular valve between stomach and duodenum
meaning.ar: البوّاب — الصمام العضلي بين المعدة والاثني عشر

terms.etymology: Greek: pylôros (πυλωρός) — gatekeeper, from pylê (gate) + ôros (guard). Pyloric stenosis in infants is a key pediatric emergency.

meaning.en: Bottom / base — the superior dome-shaped part of the stomach
meaning.ar: القاعدة / الأسفل — الجزء العلوي القبّي من المعدة

terms.etymology: Latin: fundus — bottom, base. Used in multiple organs: gastric fundus, uterine fundus, ocular fundus.

meaning.en: Folds / wrinkles of the gastric mucosa
meaning.ar: تجاعيد / طيات الغشاء المخاطي المعدي

terms.etymology: Latin: ruga (pl. rugae) — wrinkle, fold. Gastric rugae increase surface area and allow expansion.

meaning.en: Intestine / gut — internal organ of the digestive tract
meaning.ar: الأمعاء — العضو الداخلي للجهاز الهضمي

terms.etymology: Latin: intestinum — internal (from intus = within). Intestinum tenue = thin/small intestine; crassum = thick/large.

meaning.en: Twelve — named for being 12 finger-breadths long
meaning.ar: اثنا عشر — سُمّي لكونه بطول ١٢ إصبعاً

terms.etymology: Latin: duodeni — twelve each. Medieval anatomists measured it as 12 finger-breadths (~25 cm). Fixed retroperitoneal segment.

meaning.en: Empty — often found empty at autopsy
meaning.ar: فارغ — غالباً ما يوجد فارغاً في التشريح

terms.etymology: Latin: jejunus — fasting, empty. Anatomists noted it was typically empty post-mortem.

meaning.en: Twisted / coiled — the terminal part of small intestine
meaning.ar: ملتوٍ — الجزء الطرفي من الأمعاء الدقيقة

terms.etymology: Greek: eilein (εἴλειν) — to twist, roll. Note: ileum (intestine) vs ilium (hip bone) — different spelling!

meaning.en: Circular folds of the small intestinal mucosa (Kerckring's valves)
meaning.ar: الطيات الدائرية للغشاء المخاطي للأمعاء الدقيقة (صمامات كيركرينج)

terms.etymology: Latin: plica (fold) + circularis (circular). Permanent folds unlike rugae. Key X-ray finding in small bowel.

meaning.en: Blind / blind pouch — the beginning of the large intestine
meaning.ar: أعمى / كيس أعمى — بداية الأمعاء الغليظة

terms.etymology: Latin: caecus — blind, blind pouch. Called 'blind' because it has only one opening (the ileocecal valve side).

meaning.en: Sacculations of the colon between taeniae coli — key X-ray landmark
meaning.ar: تكييسات القولون بين أشرطة القولون — علامة شعاعية مهمة

terms.etymology: Latin: haustrum (pl. haustra) — bucket of a waterwheel (from haurire, to draw water). Describes the bucket-like pouches.

meaning.en: Three longitudinal muscle bands running along the colon
meaning.ar: ثلاثة أشرطة عضلية طولية تمتد على طول القولون

terms.etymology: Latin: taenia (pl. taeniae) — ribbon, band. Coli: of the colon. The three taeniae converge at the appendix base.
